response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist software craftsman with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, some Java/C++), but the resume shows minimal Kotlin, Spring, or JVM-internals depth, and no evidence of IDE/language-tooling work beyond brief C/C++ contributions to NI LabWindows/CVI a decade ago. The role requires deep JVM and Spring expertise plus genuine interest in IDE internals, which are not demonstrated here.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale systems and DX-oriented tooling
Prior IDE-adjacent C/C++ work at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, workshops, open-source tooling)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only generic Java listed in skills
No Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate or enterprise JVM framework experience shown
No recent language-tooling or IDE-internals work; recent focus is TypeScript/Node and AI tooling

key_factors.factor: Broad systems and tooling craftsmanship background
